answer:
Oral anticoagulation should be started in atrial fibrillation in patients with a CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more in men or 3 or more in women, as recommended by the ACC/AHA/ACCP/HRS Atrial Fibrillation Guideline (Anticoagulation in Atrial Fibrillation) [1]. 

References:
[1] Anticoagulation in Atrial Fibrillation, ACC/AHA/HRS, 2024

arguments.query: oral anticoagulation starting point for atrial fibrillation in patients with CHA2DS2-VASc score

result.query: oral anticoagulation starting point for atrial fibrillation in patients with CHA2DS2-VASc score

results.content: Oral anticoagulation is recommended for patients with atrial fibrillation and a CHA2DS2-VASc score of 2 or more in men or 3 or more in women. Direct oral anticoagulants are preferred over warfarin in most eligible patients.

Low-dose aspirin starting at 12-28 weeks of gestation is recommended for patients at high risk of preeclampsia. Magnesium sulfate is recommended for seizure prophylaxis in patients with severe features of preeclampsia.

For COPD patients with frequent exacerbations, LABA+LAMA combination therapy is preferred. Inhaled corticosteroids are added for patients with blood eosinophils >=300 cells/uL or continued exacerbations.
results.citation: COPD Diagnosis, Management and Prevention, GOLD, 2024

results.content: Intravenous alteplase is recommended within 4.5 hours of symptom onset for eligible patients with acute ischemic stroke. Mechanical thrombectomy is recommended for large-vessel occlusion within 24 hours in selected patients.
results.citation: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke, AHA/ASA, 2021

metadata.title: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke

results.content: In patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, an SGLT2 inhibitor is recommended for those with eGFR >=20 mL/min/1.73m2 to reduce CKD progression and cardiovascular risk. ACE inhibitors or ARBs are first-line for patients with albuminuria and hypertension.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Chronic Kidney Disease in Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

results.content: CKD staging is based on eGFR and albuminuria categories. SGLT2 inhibitors and ACE inhibitors or ARBs are recommended to slow progression in patients with albuminuric CKD, regardless of diabetes status.
results.source: KDIGO Clinical Practice Guideline for CKD
results.citation: Evaluation and Management of CKD, KDIGO, 2024

results.content: For patients with sepsis or septic shock, broad-spectrum antibiotics should be administered within 1 hour of recognition, along with 30 mL/kg of intravenous crystalloid fluid for hypotension or elevated lactate.
results.citation: Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock, Surviving Sepsis Campaign, 2021

metadata.title: Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock

results.content: High-intensity statin therapy is recommended for patients with clinical atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease and for primary prevention in adults 40-75 years with LDL-C >=190 mg/dL or diabetes with additional risk factors.

Urate-lowering therapy is recommended for patients with recurrent gout flares, tophi, or radiographic damage, with allopurinol as the preferred first-line agent, titrated to a serum urate target below 6 mg/dL.
results.source: ACR Guideline for Management of Gout

results.content: GLP-1 receptor agonists and dual GIP/GLP-1 agonists are recommended for weight management in patients with type 2 diabetes and obesity, in addition to lifestyle intervention, given their combined glycemic and weight benefits.
results.source: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.citation: Obesity Management in Type 2 Diabetes, American Diabetes Association, 2024

result.disclaimer: Evidence excerpts for professional review; not a diagnosis.
